skill.name: openapi-spec-generation
skill.description: Generate and maintain OpenAPI 3.1 specifications from code, design-first specs, and validation patterns. Use when creating API documentation, generating SDKs, or ensuring API contract compliance.
skill.summary: Create, validate, and maintain OpenAPI 3.1 specifications for REST API projects.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command, network, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found no executable skill code, no data exfiltration, and no prompt injection; most high-severity matches are false positives from OpenAPI security terminology. The remaining low risk comes from documentation examples that ask users to run package installation and generation commands.

low_findings.title: Documentation Includes Shell Command Examples
low_findings.description: The playbook contains npm installation, linting, preview, and OpenAPI generator commands. These are legitimate API documentation workflows, but users should review commands before running global package installs or generators.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present in fenced documentation examples and match the skill purpose. They are not executed by the skill, so the risk is low and user-mediated.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The detected URLs are example server entries, localhost development URLs, and links to OpenAPI-related resources. No evidence found of automatic network requests or outbound data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are static documentation and OpenAPI sample server metadata. There is no code path that sends requests to those URLs.

low_findings.title: High-Severity Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak crypto and reconnaissance alerts map to OpenAPI security scheme names, authentication descriptions, UUID formats, and validation examples. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation, system probing, or malware intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found API contract terminology and linter rule examples at representative flagged locations. The files do not contain crypto routines, reconnaissance commands, or prompt injection text.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Mediated External Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The resource file provides command-line examples for installing API tooling and generating SDKs. This is not autonomous execution, but marketplace users should treat the commands as instructions to review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is concrete, but it appears only in examples for expected OpenAPI workflows. The skill does not include scripts or executable hooks.

content.value_statement: Teams often need consistent API contracts across code, documentation, validation, and SDK generation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical OpenAPI 3.1 patterns and checks.
actual_capabilities: Create OpenAPI 3.1 specification structures for REST APIs.
Describe design-first, code-first, and hybrid documentation approaches.
Provide reusable schema, parameter, response, and security scheme patterns.
Guide validation with Spectral and Redocly configuration examples.
Show SDK generation workflows for TypeScript, Python, and Go clients.
limitations: Does not execute validation tools or generators by itself.
Requires accurate API routes, schemas, and authentication details from the user.
Examples use placeholder domains and must be adapted before publication.
Does not guarantee generated client code compiles without project-specific setup.

use_cases.title: Document a New REST API
use_cases.description: Create a clear OpenAPI 3.1 contract before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Standardize Existing Endpoints
use_cases.description: Convert route behavior and models into consistent reusable OpenAPI components.

use_cases.title: Prepare SDK Generation
use_cases.description: Review operation IDs, schemas, and responses before generating client libraries.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Spec
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new API contract.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an OpenAPI 3.1 specification outline for my REST API. Ask for missing endpoints, schemas, authentication, and server details first.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Routes to OpenAPI
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these routes and data models, then draft matching OpenAPI 3.1 paths, schemas, parameters, responses, and security requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Contract Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this OpenAPI document for missing operation IDs, reusable components, response examples, error models, and security definitions.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare SDK Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this OpenAPI 3.1 spec for SDK generation readiness across TypeScript, Python, and Go. Identify breaking risks and naming problems.

output_examples.input: A team has user, profile, and admin endpoints with bearer authentication.
output_examples.output: A structured OpenAPI plan with grouped tags, reusable user schemas, common error responses, pagination parameters, and bearer security.

output_examples.input: An existing API spec fails linting and has duplicate response definitions.
output_examples.output: A cleanup checklist that moves shared responses into components and adds clearer operation metadata.

output_examples.input: A platform team wants to generate SDKs from an OpenAPI document.
output_examples.output: A readiness review covering operation IDs, schema names, nullable fields, pagination, and language-specific client generation concerns.

best_practices: Define shared schemas, parameters, responses, and security schemes in components.
Use stable operation IDs and clear examples for every important endpoint.
Run linting and bundle checks before publishing documentation or generating SDKs.
anti_patterns: Do not publish placeholder server URLs without replacing them.
Do not mix naming conventions across paths, schemas, and operation IDs.
Do not generate SDKs before validating authentication, errors, and pagination behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete OpenAPI file?
faq.answer: It can help draft one when you provide endpoint, schema, authentication, and response details.

faq.question: Can it work from existing source code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes code-first patterns for frameworks such as FastAPI and TypeScript controller workflows.

faq.question: Does it run external tools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides example commands for user review, but the skill does not execute them.

faq.question: Which OpenAPI version does it target?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on OpenAPI 3.1 specification patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with SDK generation?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers generator preparation and examples for TypeScript, Python, and Go clients.

faq.question: Is this only for new APIs?
faq.answer: No. It also supports documenting existing APIs and improving current specifications.
